articles.title: How to get a billion passengers to fly in India
articles.summary: 📰 정부 관련 뉴스\n📢 How to get a billion passengers to fly in India...
articles.content: Mr Fareed Sheikh played it cool. “It’s my first job, and I won’t get many days off to take 22-hour train rides both ways. A flight saves time,” he told The Straits Times. From there, it would be an hour’s rail journey to Parbhani. “Now that I am taking my first flight, I believe there will be many more,” he said. That is what the government and airlines in India are banking on

articles.title: COE: Premiums rise in all categories; Category A now at $118,000
articles.summary:
📰 싱가포르, 기술 관련 뉴스
📢 COE: Premiums rise in all categories; Category A now at $118,000
articles.content: SINGAPORE — Singapore’s first Certificate of Entitlement (COE) bidding exercise of April 2026 closed on Wednesday (8 April) with premiums rising across all categories. Cat A registered the steepest increase in percentage terms, closing at $118,000. This is a 5.5% increase from the last mark of $111,890. Cat B premiums rose to $121,000, from S$115,568 in the previous exercise. Commercial vehicle premiums (Cat C) increased to $80,001. Motorcycle premiums closed at an even $10,000, up from $9,589 in the last exercise. The open category (Cat E), usually used for large cars, increased to $121,001. It was previously $118,119. It can be obtained through an online open auction conducted twice a month
